title: Hermes Agent Architecture Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the Hermes Agent codebase — the self-improving AI agent by Nous Research. Covers the core agent loop, CLI, tools system, skills, messaging gateway, and research infrastructure.

steps.title: Welcome to Hermes Agent
steps.description: Hermes Agent is a self-improving AI agent harness with tool-calling, an interactive CLI, messaging integrations, scheduled tasks, and a closed learning loop. The codebase is organized around a few key entry points and module directories. Let's walk through each layer.

steps.title: The Core Agent Loop
steps.description: The highlighted files form the heart of the system. The main agent drives the conversation loop: receiving user input, building prompts, calling the LLM, parsing tool calls, executing tools, and feeding results back. Supporting modules provide tool orchestration, persistent session state, and shared constants.

steps.title: Agent Internals
steps.description: These modules are extracted from the core loop to keep concerns separate. The prompt pipeline assembles the system prompt from identity, skills, and context. Other modules handle auto-compression, context-length tracking, prompt caching, and saving conversation trajectories for training.

steps.title: Interactive CLI.
steps.description: The interactive CLI orchestrator supports multiline editing, streaming, and slash commands. The highlighted modules handle command dispatch, the interactive setup wizard, slash-command definitions with autocomplete, interactive prompts for clarification and approval, and diagnostics.

steps.title: Tools & Environments
steps.description: Hermes ships with over fifty tool implementations. A central registry defines each tool, and a dedicated approval system handles dangerous-command detection with per-session confirmation. Terminal execution runs across seven backends — local, Docker, SSH, Daytona, Singularity, Modal, and Modal-managed — each with its own adapter.

steps.title: Skills & Learning Loop
steps.description: Bundled skill sources are organized by category — from software development and research to creative, media, and smart-home. The agent autonomously creates new skills after complex tasks and improves them during use. Skills follow the open Agent Skills standard, and dedicated tools power the creation and management loop.

steps.title: Messaging Gateway
steps.description: Hermes can communicate across seventeen platforms from a single process — including Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Matrix, Home Assistant, and enterprise chat like Feishu and WeCom. The highlighted modules handle gateway startup, cross-platform conversation continuity, DM authentication, and platform-specific adapters.

steps.title: Research & Training Infrastructure.
steps.description: Hermes Agent is research-ready. The infrastructure supports parallel batch trajectory generation, trajectory compression for training the next generation of tool-calling models, RL training environments with Atropos integration, and a dedicated CLI for reinforcement learning workflows.

steps.title: Test Suite
steps.description: The test suite mirrors the source structure with unit and integration tests covering the agent core, CLI, gateway, tools, and Honcho integration. Shared fixtures keep tests consistent.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: That's the Hermes Agent architecture: a core agent loop powered by a rich tool registry, an interactive CLI, a multi-platform messaging gateway, a self-improving skills system, and research-grade training infrastructure. Start exploring by reading the development guide or the contributor setup docs.
